antiReferences.name: Gym-bro protein RTDs
antiReferences.why: They make protein the whole identity: performance, muscle, discipline, and macro-counting. That conflicts with PEP’s social, out-of-home role.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision only. It is a different US energy/probiotic product and must never be treated as the same brand, product, or visual reference.

antiReferences.name: Clinical supplement drinks
antiReferences.why: Medical, functional, or nutrient-first cues make protein feel strange in bars, restaurants, dates, and festivals.

antiReferences.name: Energy drink culture
antiReferences.why: High-stimulation, extreme-performance, or late-night fuel language pulls PEP away from refreshing social pleasure.

antiReferences.name: Kids’ juice or childish soda cues
antiReferences.why: PEP can be fruity and fun, but it must remain adult, premium, and orderable in hospitality.

antiReferences.name: PROTOCHOL-style protein-alcohol fusion
antiReferences.why: It connects protein with alcohol and novelty fitness culture, while PEP should be a better social drink without relying on alcohol.

audience: Socially active adults looking for a more exciting option when out
People reducing or skipping alcohol without wanting the occasion to feel flat
Taste-led consumers who choose drinks for flavour, mood, and experience
Aesthetic, brand-sensitive customers in bars, restaurants, hotels, festivals, and events
Consumers open to protein as an added feel-good benefit, not as a gym requirement
brandFitRules: Approve concepts that make PEP feel like something people order when going out.
Reject concepts that make PEP feel like a gym, supplement, recovery, or macro-tracking product.
Taste, refreshment, occasion, and fun must appear before protein in hierarchy and messaging.
Protein should be framed as a surprising bonus inside a light social drink, not the core ritual.
PEP should feel adult, premium, playful, and hospitality-ready.
Use social proof, serve moments, and flavour range energy to build desirability.
Borrow social ritual cues from cocktails and mocktails, but do not imitate alcohol brands too literally.
Keep the brand anchored to pep-drink.com and never associate it with drinkpep.com.

designPatterns: Mood and occasion are central to the brand story
Premium social drink positioning without alcohol
Designed to feel appropriate before, during, or after social gatherings
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong: non-alcoholic, functional, social beverage; closest strategic bridge to PEP’s protein-plus-social challenge.
inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: A strong peer because it makes function feel emotional and social rather than clinical. PEP can learn from its ability to sell mood and occasion first.

designPatterns: Premium presentation for adult social settings
Broad serve versatility across cocktails and mixed drinks
Clear replacement role for classic social drinks
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong social fit, partial format fit: non-alcoholic social drinking is direct, but spirits are not protein refreshment drinks.
inLineBrands.name: Lyre's
inLineBrands.why: Relevant for showing how an alcohol-free product can feel adult, social, and premium instead of like a compromise.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong social fit, partial product fit: it replaces alcohol in social serves, but the liquid format and flavour logic differ from PEP.
inLineBrands.name: Seedlip
inLineBrands.why: An iconic reference for making non-alcoholic ordering feel legitimate in high-end venues. PEP should borrow the confidence, not the exact spirits codes.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong: non-alcoholic, social, flavour-led drink with direct mocktail competition relevance.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Mocktails
inLineBrands.why: A direct benchmark for adult non-alcoholic social drinking in the Dutch and European context.

designPatterns: Premium soft-drink role in social occasions
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Good: non-alcoholic social mixer and adult soft drink, though not protein-led.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Ginger Ale
inLineBrands.why: Useful because it shows how a non-alcoholic soft drink can still feel adult and venue-appropriate rather than childish or purely functional.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: Use only for web structure inspiration, not brand look
Clear product-detail sections for benefits, flavour, and purchase path
Simple navigation from hero product to full range

oneLiner: A premium protein drink for going out: fruity, refreshing, social, and made to feel good while having fun.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: functional vitamin drink, not a social hospitality drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional-protein direction because it leads with health, vitamins, and protein utility.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP social should not feel like a nutrient-delivery tool or routine supplement.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: hydration mix, not a ready social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional hydration and exercise-adjacent use cases.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its buying logic is rational and performance-oriented, while PEP social is emotional and occasion-led.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: BCAA/energy drink rather than social refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional sports and energy territory.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It would pull PEP toward athletic performance, stimulation, and gym culture.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Misleading: protein plus alcohol is a novelty fusion, not PEP’s better non-alcoholic social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Mapped to functional protein because protein is central and the alcohol angle is niche.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP should not imply alcohol, post-workout drinking, or protein-as-stunt positioning.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: functional hydration, not social ritual.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional hydration and vitamin-led benefit territory.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its role is replenishment and everyday function, while PEP’s role is social experience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: refreshing soda format, but wellness and gut-health logic dominate.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade because it frames the drink as a healthier everyday soda alternative.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP should not lead with gut health or everyday wellness routine.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate-to-good: non-alcoholic aperitif context overlaps, but its core is a refined lifestyle upgrade.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Mapped to lifestyle because natural sophistication and everyday mindful choice are more central than high-energy social fun.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful category awareness, but PEP should be more playful, fruity, and event-ready.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: soda format and flavour appeal, but wellness benefit dominates.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle wellness because prebiotic health is central.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP must not become a gut-health soda; protein is secondary to social pleasure.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: flavour-led soda energy, but gut-health lifestyle is primary.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade because it sells a healthier daily soda identity.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP should be ordered for nights out and group occasions, not primarily for everyday wellness.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: kombucha can act as a premium soft-drink alternative, but wellness craft leads.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Yaya Kombucha
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in the source, but it leans toward premium craft and lifestyle wellness rather than PEP’s selected social-drink lane.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more artisanal and wellness-coded than festival, bar, and event-led.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: botanical soda and kombucha can replace alcohol, but craft fermentation is the main story.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Walker Brothers
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in the source, but it leans craft, botanical, and community-focused rather than PEP’s fruity social ritual.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP should not anchor its identity in fermentation, provenance, or craft complexity.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: refreshing and flavourful, but kombucha wellness leads.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade because it centres wellness, balance, and approachable kombucha.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP social should not feel like a probiotic wellness habit.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: canned refreshment format, but mood-calming daily wellness dominates.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade because it targets relaxation, stress relief, and wellness identity.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP should be more outgoing and social, less calm and routine-based.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Good occasion fit but not active because the source maps it to lifestyle and its format can include alcohol.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs mainly to lifestyle upgrade in the CSV because it is positioned as convenient premium RTD enjoyment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful as inspiration for flavour-led celebration, but PEP should stay non-alcoholic and protein-refreshment specific.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Good social category fit, but the source maps it to lifestyle.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade because it focuses on mindful drinking, authenticity, and premium adult alternatives.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is close, but PEP needs a more fun, fruity, festival-ready expression rather than a classic alcohol-replacement identity.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: functional soda format, but health-led soda replacement dominates.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: more fiz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade because it leads with sugar-free, fibre-enriched, healthy soda cues.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP must avoid becoming primarily a better-for-you soda with nutrition claims at the centre.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak-to-moderate: direct protein drink format, but mapped to functional protein.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Juuz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional protein because protein content, sugar-free claims, and product function are central.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Even though the product type is closer to PEP, the brand logic is too functional for the social direction.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weak product fit: alcoholic cider, not non-alcoholic protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Rekorderlig
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strong social occasion fit despite drink-type mismatch.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Alcoholic fruit cider competes in the same social moment but is not PEP’s product format or alcohol-free role.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for fruit-led social desirability, easy outdoor occasion energy, premium refreshment cues, and a range system where flavour is instantly understood.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weak product fit: alcoholic cocktail RTD.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: On The Rocks Cocktails
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strong bar-style and premium social fit despite being alcoholic.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Alcoholic cocktail format; PEP should borrow the hospitality confidence, not the alcohol-led proposition.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for bar-inspired credibility, premium ready-to-serve confidence, clear serve occasion, and an adult layout feel that makes canned drinks acceptable in elevated venues.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Partial fit: RTD social format with mocktail relevance, but not protein-led and can be alcoholic.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ole cocktails
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Source maps it to lifestyle upgrade, but it carries strong social and event cues.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Partly alcoholic cocktail territory and not a protein refreshment drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for celebratory RTD energy, flavour-led hierarchy, party and outdoor-event relevance, and convenient premium serve culture.

positioningStatement: PEP is positioned as the better social drink: a premium protein refreshment for bars, restaurants, festivals, dates, rooftops, and group moments, replacing alcohol or dull soft drinks with a fruity, feel-good ritual.

visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe, fruity
visual.mood: High-energy, social, flavour-forward, premium-but-approachable, joyful, outgoing, and refreshing. It should feel made for holding in hand at a bar, festival, dinner, or rooftop event.
visual.photography: Show real social occasions: friends at tables, hands holding the drink, outdoor gatherings, venue serves, movement, laughter, date-night moments, and flavour-led refreshment. Avoid gym scenes, solo routine shots, clinical ingredient layouts, and overly staged wellness still life.
rejectLooks: Chaotic party clutter that feels cheap
Desk, gym, or routine-led product worlds

voice.tone: Social, playful, premium, confident, light, and taste-led. Talk like a stylish friend inviting someone into a better night out, not like a coach or supplement label.
